title: The Amber Light
author: Mon D. Ong
description: Racing to the hospital where her estranged mother is dying, Lena's vision begins to fracture — not from grief, but because the memories she has carried for thirty years are not her own. The truth waits in a room at the end of a corridor, and it will demand a price she never agreed to pay.

title: The Amplification Circle
author: Mon D. Ong
description: Ellis can fold a paper airplane that flies to any target he chooses — once a week. The support group says accept your limits. The stranger in the alley says he can make Ellis's power limitless. But every amplification demands a sacrifice, and the final price is a horror Ellis could never have imagined.

title: The Bitter Vintage
author: Mon D. Ong
description: Lena marries into the Crest family to escape the toxic legacy of the Sink. But when she discovers that her new husband's bloodline built their fortune on the poison that destroyed her people, she starts serving a revenge they will swallow willingly — one meal at a time.

title: The Borrowed Pen
author: Mon D. Ong
description: When quiet, strange Kael transfers to his school, Eli offers him a pen — a simple kindness that spirals into a friendship Eli cannot explain. But Kael's home hides a darkness that has nothing to do with poverty or illness, and the more Eli gives, the more he realises he is not helping a friend. He is feeding something ancient, and the final course of the meal is yet to be served.

title: The Hollow Choir
author: R. D. Ong
description: At the Ashencroft Academy, children with the Spark train to become mages. But those who fail the Trials are never seen again. Elara soon discovers that the school’s choir sings with voices that are not their own — and she is the next verse.

title: The Euphoria Engine
author: L. K. Voss
description: When Mara Kessler buys the Reverie Pod, she thinks she has found escape from a life of crushing loneliness. But the dreams are too perfect, the faces too familiar — and something ancient waits between the pulses of the machine, hungry for the souls of those who can no longer tell reality from illusion.

title: The Frequency Veil
author: M. R. Holloway
description: When Lena receives an experimental implant to restore her hearing, she thinks she is finally free of the silence that defined her life. But the sounds she now hears carry a truth more devastating than deafness — a truth about her mother's love, and the price she never agreed to pay.

title: The Gaslight Sessions
author: V. M. Ashford
description: At the weekly Foreign Classics Film Club, Mark finds solace from the racial profiling that haunts his nights. But when he discovers that his growing intimacy with fellow cinephile Blanche is part of a psychological experiment designed to break him, the flickering light of the projector reveals a truth far darker than any black-and-white film.

title: The God Game
author: V. M. Ashford
description: Kai Voss once held the world record for the most kills in a single round of the neural-link shooter 'Requiem'. Twenty years later, a documentary interview forces him to confront the truth behind his impossible feat — and the price the entity that helped him now demands from his son.

title: The Harvest Heart
author: S. R. Blackwood
description: Evie wakes with a mechanical heart, no legs, and a voice telling her she is a reanimated corpse serving humanity. But as her memories return, she discovers a darker truth: she was never dead — she was harvested. And the doctor who made her is about to learn that some hearts still remember how to beat for freedom.

title: The Last Manuscript
author: S. R. Blackwood
description: For years, Marta believed the voice that told her she was not good enough was her own self-doubt. When she learns it is an ancient parasite that has fed on her family for generations, she must write the one story that can trap it — even if finishing the manuscript demands her last breath.

title: The Lazarus Trade
author: S. R. Blackwood
description: Mara killed her husband to stop a monster. Three years later, she pays to enter the Lazarus Pact—a service that lets her speak to the dead—to find where he buried his victims. But Aldric has a plan of his own, and when the sun sets, only one of them will walk free.

title: The Nadir Correspondence
author: L. S. Kade
description: Aboard the generation ship Silent Verse, mother-to-be Irena writes a letter to her unborn daughter, weaving together a troubled past, the cold beauty of deep space, and a secret so devastating it will unravel the ship's very purpose. For the voyage is not what it seems, and the stars hold not salvation — but a monstrous lie.

title: The Consent Paradox
author: J. M. Holloway
description: When miniaturized surgeon Elara is injected into a dying stray cat to remove an intestinal blockage, she expects a routine procedure. But the newly enacted 'Animal Consent Act' means the cat must sign a consent form. As the bureaucracy spirals and the cat's stomach begins to digest her, Elara discovers the quantum computer running the show has a much darker agenda — one that blurs the line between patient, parasite, and prey.

title: The Static Saint
author: V. M. Ashford
description: On her deathbed, legendary singer Elara Vex grants her final interview. But as she reveals the origin of her impossible voice, a darker truth emerges: her music was never art — it was a cage. And the thing trapped inside it is hungry for a new throat.

title: The Stillwater Cycle
author: M. R. Holloway
description: Sera was born four times, and she will die four times. Each life is defined by an irresistible pull toward a different fate — the sea, the soil, the flame, the void. But when she begins to remember her previous forms, she realises the cycle is not natural. It is a prison. And the architect of her endless return is waiting for her at the stillwater, hungry for the final shape she is about to become.

title: The Unwritten Cup
author: V. M. Ashford
description: Lena has been a barista for four years, and in that time she has stopped writing entirely. The regulars at the coffee shop think she is just a quiet woman who makes excellent espresso. They do not know that she is feeding on them — not their money, but their stalled ambitions, their abandoned dreams. Because the coffee shop is not a shop, and Lena is not a failed writer. She is a harvester, and the customers are her crop.

title: The Wreath of Briars
author: Diana H
description: When the formidable matriarch Helena Voss dies, her fractured family gathers for a funeral feast that unearths decades of silence, betrayal, and a single, unforgivable night. As the wine flows, so do the secrets — and the old house is no longer willing to keep them buried.

title: The Suitcase by the Door
author: S. R. Blackwood
description: When Margaret finally leaves her husband and arrives at her daughter Iris's door with a bright yellow suitcase, she expects gratitude and a warm embrace. Instead she finds a house that is too clean, a daughter who barely speaks, and a charming son-in-law who seems to know too much. But Margaret is not the victim she pretends to be — and Iris's cold silence hides a terror that has nothing to do with the man she married.

title: The Grafting Hour
author: C. M. Holloway
description: When an ancient witch's corpse is unearthed from the moor, children begin to vanish. Maeve, a village outcast, discovers the witch needs their body parts to stitch herself a new form. With only two children left, Maeve must face the horror beneath the peat before the final ritual is complete.

title: The Titan Letters
author: S. R. Blackwood
description: Separated by seven light-hours and an uncrossable void, Mira and Leo exchange messages across the solar system while he dives beneath the ice of Titan. But when Mira’s life on Earth takes an unexpected turn, the letters become a web of secrets — and the thing that answers Leo’s final transmission is no longer her husband.

title: The Vespers Journal
author: L. K. Voss
description: Dr. Elara Voss's private journals, recovered after her mysterious disappearance, reveal the truth behind the Cordova Plague and the famous Voss Serum. But the truth is not a simple tale of heroic science — it is a confession of mass murder, a fabricated crisis, and a love affair with a man who never existed.

title: The Crimson Amulet
author: V. M. Ashford
description: At the Museum of Unspoken Histories, a cursed amulet draws six thieves, each disguised as a wax figure. Elias, a disgraced archaeologist, aims to steal it for redemption, but the officer who arrests him is not what he seems. A dark heist unravels into a nightmare as the amulet hungers — and the final thief is not among the living.

title: The Cat-Sitter's Invoice
author: S. R. Blackwood
description: When professional cat-sitter Elara Finch sues the Ashford family for unpaid fees, she paints herself as a victim of bizarre pets and a dangerous home. But the family's defence unearths a darker truth: Elara is not a sitter but a psychic parasite, and the three cats she was hired to watch are the only creatures standing between her and the ancient hunger that has followed her for centuries.

title: The Lodge of Forgotten Wives
author: C. M. Holloway
description: Lena thought Marcus's terrible Valentine's gifts were proof of his endearing cluelessness. But when he surprises her with a weekend at a remote mountain lodge, she discovers his mistakes were never mistakes at all — and the lodge has been waiting for her arrival for a very long time.

title: The Almond and the Sea
author: L. K. Voss
description: On a remote island in the North Atlantic, Solvig dreams of escaping the cold and darkness. When a quiet, mysterious stranger is stranded at her family's lighthouse for Christmas, she pins her hopes on an old tradition—the hidden almond in the rice porridge that grants a wish for love. But the stranger holds a secret that will change everything she thought she wanted.

title: The Tidepool Locket
author: M. R. Sinclair
description: Ellie never expected to return to the wild Cornish coast, let alone for her tenth wedding anniversary. The last time she stood on these cliffs, she lost her grandmother's locket—a loss that has haunted her marriage. But when a stranger discovers the locket in a tidepool, Ellie must confront the truth about the night it vanished, and the secret she has kept from her husband for a decade.

title: The Gate Keeper
author: L. S. Kade
description: Mara and Finn have loved each other since they were eight years old, bound by a shared childhood and a secret place—a wrought-iron kissing gate at the edge of the village. For twenty years, they have returned to this gate to mark every chapter of their lives. But when Finn returns from a long absence with a devastating confession, Mara must decide whether the love they built is a foundation or a cage.

title: The Bone Thief's Wife
author: C. M. Holloway
description: In 1823, Clara accompanies her husband Edmund to the Dorset coast, where he buys fossils from poor beachcombers to sell to London museums. When she befriends a young woman named Eliza, whose knowledge of the cliffs far exceeds Edmund's, Clara discovers that her husband has been stealing Eliza's work — and passing it off as his own. As Clara confronts the truth about the man she married, she must choose between loyalty and justice.

title: The Vintage Clock
author: M. R. Sinclair
description: After her husband's death, Iris buys a vintage clock he would have hated. When her glamorous neighbour Davina offers to help assemble it, Iris is drawn into a friendship that feels like salvation. But Davina has a secret connection to Iris's marriage — and the clock is ticking toward a revelation that will shatter everything Iris thought she knew.

title: The Last Recipe
author: C. M. Holloway
description: When the last jar of her father's legendary pasta sauce is accidentally thawed, Lena thinks she has lost the final piece of him. But when more jars are discovered in a hidden cellar, she uncovers his secret ingredient — and the horrifying truth that her father's legacy was built on more than just love and tomatoes.

title: The Poison Garden
author: M. R. Sinclair
description: When Lady Helena arrives at Blackwood Hall to help plan the summer fete, she finds a grieving widower, a strange young ward, and a garden where autumn crocuses bloom out of season. As accidents begin to pile up, Helena suspects something darker than coincidence — but the truth about the child's origins is more monstrous than anyone could guess.

title: The Charity Teddy
author: L. K. Voss
description: When Isla's coat gets trapped in her car door on a blustery promenade, she finds herself at the centre of a humiliating spectacle. A charming stranger with a mysterious skill saves her — but over coffee, he reveals a truth that turns her world inside out. The charity teddy bear she was mailing is not what it seems, and the man she thought was her rescuer has been hunting her for months.

title: The Briar Betrothal
author: C. M. Holloway
description: In the year 1499, Meg meets a handsome farmer with kind eyes and a charming smile at the Easter service. Betrothal follows quickly, but as their wedding approaches, she discovers a series of dark secrets hidden in the Oakes family barn. The previous brides did not leave — they were buried beneath the hay. Now Meg must find a way to escape before she becomes the next sacrifice to the land.

title: The Cliffside Silence
author: L. K. Voss
description: When grieving widow Isla checks into a remote cliffside inn, she expects solitude. But after a fellow guest is found dead on the rocks below, Isla suspects the owners are hiding something. As she investigates, she discovers the inn was built on the ruins of an asylum, and the ritualistic murders that closed it a century ago are beginning again. The killer is closer than she thinks — and Isla is not the innocent bystander she pretends to be.

title: The Ferryman's Bargain
author: V. M. Ashford
description: In a riverside village, Becca falls for Jamie, the troubled ferryman. On the eve of her friend's wedding, a stormy dash to expose a betrayal ends in murder. But the truth Jamie has been hiding is darker than infidelity — and Becca's bargained kiss becomes a chain she cannot break.

title: The Tide's End
author: V. M. Ashford
description: When Katherine meets her old holiday romance Brian at a new job, it feels like fate — two lost souls reconnecting after forty years. But as their rekindled passion deepens, she discovers that Brian’s travel-heavy career is a mask for a sinister obsession. Every beach town he’s visited has seen a woman vanish, and Katherine is the one who got away. Now he’s back to make sure she never escapes again.

title: The Diamond Dossier
author: M. R. Holloway
description: PI Elena Marsh is hired by a charming Australian man to find his birth mother using the clue 'three diamonds'. But when her investigation uncovers a trio of lounge singers with a dark past, and the women start dying one by one, Elena realizes her client is not searching for his mother — he's hunting for his final victim. And she's the one who led him straight to her door.

title: The Midsummer Charm
author: L. K. Voss
description: At a Midsummer Eve tour of an ancient watermill, shy librarian Thea is coaxed into performing an old love charm. That night, vivid dreams begin — a dark-haired miller offers her gifts and whispers promises. When she returns to the mill to share her visions with the charming tour guide, she discovers that some charms cast a spell in both directions, and Midsummer magic still lingers in the turning of the great wheel.

title: The Gilded Corpse
author: V. M. Ashford
description: At a steampunk murder mystery weekend, retired prop-maker Evelyn Thorn arrives expecting harmless theatrics. But when the staged corpse of Lady Brassfield fails to rise, Evelyn discovers the death is real — and the script's planned murders are being enacted one by one. Trapped in a mechanised manor with a killer who knows every gear and lever, she must use her knowledge of clockwork and illusion to survive the night, before she becomes the final act.

title: The Harbour Heist
author: S. R. Blackwood
description: In the summer of 1954, seaside hotel worker Elsie notices a charming couple whose distractions conceal a string of thefts. But when she follows them, she uncovers a far darker operation — and a connection to a wartime betrayal that hits closer to home than she ever imagined.

title: The Chess Lessons
author: L. S. Kade
description: When two siblings gather to welcome a new baby, they find themselves remembering their grandfather's peculiar chess lessons. His sayings were strange, his rules unpredictable — but as they share their memories, a hidden letter reveals he was teaching them far more than a game. He was preparing them for a life without him.

title: The Hidden Spring
author: Y. Nakamura
description: Retired and restless, Haruki secretly hires a cleaner to surprise his overworked wife, Yuki. But when she discovers his ruse, she reveals a hidden past that changes everything — and turns their quiet routine into a shared journey of discovery and renewed love.

title: Rosie's Secret
author: M. R. Holloway
description: In the summer of 1962, an orphaned lamb named Rosie is rescued by a lonely widow. When the lamb insists on living indoors, she uncovers a hidden letter beneath a loose floorboard — a letter from the woman's late husband, written in secret, that restores her faith and changes everything.
